comments: No Friday Night Connection for the next few weeks as Tommy is going on holiday.
Tony Wilson "And you can't have 3 weeks off as well, I shall expect to see you here next Friday."

track_listing.track: Over The Rainbow / Kill The King

transcript.text: On 275 and 285, also in stereo on VHF, this is National Radio 1.
TV on the radio here, Thomas the Vance, and welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
And this evening, we with joy and delight rebroadcast the 45-minute set that exploded into the atmosphere last year at Reading.
And the one that I'm talking about is by Mr. David Coverdale and Whitesnake.
Got some classic cuts by Led Zeppelin, Status Quo, Japan, Dixie Drakes and Van de Graaff Generator, among others.
And new releases by Girl, Ted Newton, Sniff on the Tears, Queen, Girl, School and Praying Mantis.
And the answer to last week's Friday Night Connection.
Not a new one, though. Tonight, I'll explain all that later.
But this evening, we start with this.

Oh, and I was handed something a couple of weeks ago, which I should have read out a couple of weeks ago,
but I've only just found it in my wallet, along with my last remaining 10-bob note.
Best wishes to Jimmy the Tyrant Norcott of Dagenham from all his mates.
Seven minutes to eleven and it's time for the Friday Night Connection.
Wrong music you may be thinking.
You're wrong yourself.
Stick around and see.
Last week we played you Beneath, Between and Behind by Rush from the LP Fly By Night.
Running With The Devil by Van Halen and Firefly by Uriah Heep.

Now, Mr. Studley, that's Mark, the you and I, has put together a Friday Night Connection,
but we're not going to be doing it for about a month, in fact, for exactly a month,
because I'm away, they're sending me away since about three weeks.
Next week we've got a Genesis special, two hours of Genesis as recorded, recently in London.
The week after that, Ian Gillan is going to be hosting the Friday Rock Show,
and the week after that, we will have a repeat of The Wall,
